titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Vercellensis
VL3
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

msItem.title: Quattuor Euangelia
Four Gospels in order Mt Jo Lc Mc

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

listBibl.bibl: Editions by Irico and Bianchini in the 18th century, also: A.F. Gasquet, Codex Vercellensis. (Collectanea Biblica Latina 3). Rome 1914.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription covers pages 181-321. The transcription includes foliation, lineation, all punctuation and abbreviations. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Gasquet distinguishes between readings no longer visible but presented in the two earlier editions of the manuscript by Irico and Bianchini (recorded in the transcription as "IricoBianchini"), and text supplied by Gasquet himself. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Error in 1:32 fixed by Hugh Houghton (print edition is correct).
Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Change to verse division in 5:18-19 by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: Gasquet marks 'lo et' as no longer visible, but it is clear on our photographs.
Gasquet marks 'ante' as no longer visible, but it is clear on our photographs.
Gasquet does not record the correction, which is written in small letters in the left hand margin
umquam] unquam Gasquet and Irico, but 'umquam' can be made out from the photographs.
Gasquet marks 'ad eum ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'rosolymis ' as no longer visible, but it is clear on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'tem ' as no longer visible, but it is clear on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'tas ut ' as no longer visible, but it can just about be made out on our photographs
qui] quis Irico; the manuscript clearly has 'qui'
profeta] propheta Bianchini; an 'f' can be seen in the manuscript.
Neither Gasquet or Irico records the superline, visible from the manuscript (cf. John 1:36)
Gasquet marks 'e ' of 'erat ' as no longer visible, but it is clear on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'am ' as no longer visible, but it is clear on our photographs
Gasquet does not mark the hanging line
Gasquet records a hanging line and a capital letter, which images of the ms show to be incorrect. Furthermore, the ms looks like it reads illu¬ rather than illum, although Irico has illum
Gasquet marks 'il ' of 'illi ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks a hanging line and capital 'e' on 'ecce', neither of which is found in the photos of the manuscript.
this space is not recorded by Gasquet, but is clear from the images
Gasquet marks 'chi ' of 'architriclinus ' as no longer visible, but it can be clearly made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'a ' of 'aqua ' as no longer visible, but it can be clearly made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'num ' of 'uinum ' as no longer visible, but it can be clearly made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'et n ' as no longer visible, but it can be clearly made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'unde ' as no longer visible, but it can be clearly made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks this line as no longer visible, but it can be made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'riati fue ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'aute¬ ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'et ma ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'pascha ' as no longer visible, but it can just be made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks this line as no longer visible, but it can be made out on our photographs
uendentes IricoGasquet marks 'dentes ' as no longer visible, but it can be clearly made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'hoc ' as no longer visible, but it can be clearly made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'et ' as no longer visible, but it can be clearly made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'nomine ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks this as a hanging line
ih¬m has been re-inked by a later hand; it may be a correction.
The photos suggest that the final 'm ' of natum may have been added by a different hand
'Bianchini sic habet: natum est de{|spiritu spiritus es
The correction is not recorded by Gasquet or Irico; see also line 14
Gasquet marks this line as no longer visible, but it can be made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks 'creditis ' as no longer visible, and follows the reading of Irico and Bianchini. The word can be seen in our photographs and, furthermore, is 'credetis '.
Gasquet marks 'Re ' of 'Respondit ' as no longer visible, but it can be clearly made out on our photographs
Gasquet marks this line as no longer legible, but 'propter u ' can be made out from our images
et nunc est additio 2m forte saec. IX
The images of the manuscript are slightly unclear for this word, and suggest that 'accepistis ' may have been corrected to 'accipistis '
Gasquet marks only the 's ' as visible, but 'hs ' and the superline can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks the 'r ' of 'mare ' as no longer legible, but it can be made out from our images
Gasquet omits the hanging line, which is clear from the images
Gasquet omits the hanging line, which is clear from the images
Gasquet marks the 't ' of 'multae ' as no longer legible, but it can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks this line as no longer legible, but 'set ' can be made out from our images
sic Gasquet; patre{|et didi cerit Bianchini; patre et|}didicerit Irico
Gasquet marks the final 'unt ' of this line as no longer legible, but it can be clearly made out from our images
Gasquet marks the 'mod ' of 'quomodo ' as no longer legible, but all except the 'm ' can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks the 'hi ' of 'hic ' as no longer legible, but it can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks the 'ma ' of 'manducare ' as no longer legible, but it can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks this word as no longer legible, but 'lis ' can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks a hanging line with capital 'T '. This is not supported by our images, although only the initial 't ' can be made out on this line

sabbotao] sabbato Gasquet; sabboteo Irico (not mentioned by Gasquet). The images of the manuscript support 'sabbotao ' ( 'sabbato ' in line 19 is clear, but compare line 2 of page 230); the repetition of 'cir ' is correct
Gasquet and Irico read 'sabbato ', but 'sabboato ' is clear from images of the manuscript. Compare line 13 of page 229.
Gasquet marks this line as no longer legible, but 'te ... mue ' can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks the 'uen ' of 'uenerat ' as no longer legible, but the 'n ' can be made out from our images
Gasquet does not mark these spaces, which are visible in images of the manuscript
sacesrdotes] sacerdotes Gasquet and Irico; the 's ' is clearly visible in images of the manuscript
conprhehenderent] conprehenderunt Gasquet and Irico; the 'h ' is clearly visible on images of the manuscript
Gasquet omits the hanging line and capital 'R ', which are both clear from the images
Note; this column has 25 lines; there is no trace of the final line on our images.
uere li {| beri] uere |} liberi Gasquet and Irico; the manuscript is clear
Irico reads habemus: hahemus appears to be a typographical error in Gasquet
Gasquet does not mark a hanging line or capital; the images show that the 'A ' is a capital, and it appears to project into the margin compared with the lines below
Gasquet marks 'meum ' as no longer legible, but it can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks this line as no longer legible, but it can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks the 'hon ' of 'honorificatis ' as no longer legible, but it can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks 'amen di ' as no longer legible, but it can be made out from our images
Gasquet marks the whole line as no longer legible, but 'o uob ' can be made out from our images
Gasquet omits the hanging line and capital 'D ', which are clearly visible from images of the manuscript
Gasquet marks the 'ni ' of 'manifestetur ' as no longer legible, but it can be made out from our images
-mo qui dicitur ih¬s tetigit inter lineas minoribus litteris scripta -- Gasquet
Gasquet omits the hanging line and capital 'A ', which are clearly visible from images of the manuscript
Gasquet omits the hanging line and capital 'I ', which are clearly visible from images of the manuscript
disci {| puli] discipu |} li Gasquet and Irico, but the spacing on the manuscript seems clear
Gasquet marks the whole line as no longer legible, but the hanging 'A ' can be made out from our images
Irico sed {| estro for est |} latro
The manuscript images of this line appear to read '..manua ', but the reading is not altogether secure
Gasquet and Irico both read 'propriae ', but the 'h ' is clearly visible from the manuscript
Gasquet marks the whole of 'rapit ' as unreadable, but at least the first four letters can be made out from photographs
Gasquet marks the 'par ' of 'dispargit ' as unreadable, but the 'pa ' can be made out on images
It is possible that this is a hanging line, but the evidence from the photographs and Irico is ambivalent
Note that Irico and Bianchini regard the last two lines as illegible. It is possible, however, that they were never written, or were erased

B. dixit illi iesus e{|go sum resurrect . . . ui|}credit in me etsi, I. ut supra, quod probabilius uidetur si attendas linearum numerum in unaquaque columna reperiendum; sin autem ad sensum referatur, probabilius est uerba *sum resurrectio qui credit* inter lineas addita fuisse quod in ms. non amplius uideri potest.
Gasquet does not mark these spaces, which can be seen in the manuscript
sursum Irico and Gasquet, susum Bianchini; see further Schaefer, Theologische Revue 62 (1966) 374-6
Gasquet omits the hanging line and capital 'F ', which are clearly visible from images
The correction is not recorded by Gasquet or Irico, but is clear from images: this is one of the best-preserved pages of the ms.
mi{|nister Bianchini is correct: this is one of the best-preserved pages, despite Gasquet following Irico with min|}ister.
Irico et Bianchini turba quae stabat
'esseias ' is a typographical error in Gasquet; Irico has 'eseias ', which can be clearly read on this well-preserved page of the manuscript
'occulos ' is a typographical error in Gasquet and Irico; 'oculos ' can be clearly read on this well-preserved page of the manuscript
Bianchini . . . m illis; Irico . . . in illis
Gasquet does not record the hanging line or capital 'D ', which can be clearly seen in the image
A mark visible on the image of the ms suggests that the 'd ' may have been deleted
sp¬m Irico, but sp¬u is still clearly visible in ms.
'1m testifi{|tus, cum B. corr. 2m testifi|}catus cum I. '
uo{|bis Bianchini is correct: uobis|} in Irico and Gasquet is wrong
Gasquet marks 'eius res ' as no longer visible, but it is clear on our images of the manuscript
Gasquet marks 'be ' of 'recumbens ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out in our images of the manuscript
Gasquet marks 'ex dis ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out in our images of the manuscript
Gasquet marks 'nc ' of 'intinctum ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out in our images of the manuscript
Gasquet marks 'nem ' of 'panem ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out in our images of the manuscript
Gasquet marks 'porrexero ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out in our images of the manuscript
Gasquet marks 'it er ' of 'intinxit ergo ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out in our images of the manuscript
Gasquet marks 'dit iu ' of 'tradidit iudas ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out in our images of the manuscript
Gasquet does not record the hanging line and capital 'D ', which are clearly visible in images of the manuscript; Irico reads 'dixit ' for 'Dicit '

ab.w:
di
No apparent running title
xissem

ab.note: Gasquet and Irico both read 'quae ', but 'que ' is all that can be made out from our fairly clear image of this page

Gasquet and Irico simply read 'non uide" on this line, but in between these two words there is a gap, which appears from images to be filled by a fairly clear letter 'm ', with no obvious bleed-through on the verso
Gasquet and Irico just read 'quia ', but there is clearly a 'd ' and possibly an 'i ' following visible on images of the manuscript
Gasquet and Irico both read 'eo ', but 'ego ' is visible on images of the manuscript

ab.w:
amplio
No apparent running title
rem

ab.note: Gasquet and Irico both read 'uos | persequentur ', but the division of the words is clear from image sof the manuscript
Gasquet omits the hanging line and capital 'C ', both of which are clearly visible from the manuscript
Gasquet omits et, reading offerret, Bianchini has offere et
Gasquet and Irico both read 'pusillum ', but 'pusillu¬ ' is reasonably clear from the images.
Irico tristitiam uestram, Bianchini tristitia uestra; Gasquet 's comment 'in ms. m est expunct. ' has been taken to refer to both occurrences.

ab.w:
msic Irico, Bianchini tu | me
e

ab.note: Gasquet 's 'illlis ' appears to be a typographical error; Irico has illis

'I. habet in semetipsis in una linea, et tunc columna tantum 23 lineas habet; B. uerisimilius scribit in duabus lineis ut supra. ' This raises the question; how much could Gasquet actually see, bearing in mind he claims here *not* simply to be reproducing either editor?
Gasquet does not mark the hanging line and capital 'I ', both of which are clear from images of the manuscript
Irico perperam legit ministros uenit ibi cum in una linea; B. cum ms.
Gasquet also marks 'tra ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out on images of the manuscript
Gasquet also marks 'erat ' as no longer visible, but it can be made out on images of the manuscript
Irico si respondis, Bianchini si . . . respondis
occidere Bianchini; the faint images of the manuscript support occideret
praetorium Bianchini; the ms is clear
Gasquet marks 'et ego ' as no longer visible, but 'et .go ' can be fairly well made out on images of the manuscript
ueritate Bianchini; the ms images are clear
caluariae Irico; calbari.. can be seen in the ms
Gasquet does not record a hanging line; the manuscript is not entirely clear, but it seems probable given the beginnings of the next few lines
Gasquet reads 'tollerentur ', but Irico 's reading 'tollerenttur ' can be verified from the manuscript
Irico illi s{|criptura, Bianchini illi . . . s|}criptura

It is highly improbable that Gasquet could see any more of this page than the first four lines, given that that was all he could see of the other side, and seems to be all that survives of this page. It seems likely that the italic typesetting was omitted from this page. And yet he comments in verse 16 that Bianchini reads 'rhabbi ' "cum ms." even though Irico could not see the end of this word.

ab.w:
ascen
As observed opposite, it seems unlikely that Gasquet could read more than the first four lines of this columndo

ab.note: Gasquet omits the hanging line and capital 'T ', which can be clearly seen in the manuscript
Gasquet omits the hanging line and capital 'P ', which can be clearly seen in the manuscript

Unlike page 314, the first fifteen or so lines of this column are still extant, and were perhaps visible to Gasquet. The only letters which can be securely made out from our images are 'dis ' of discipulis in line 10.
See note above. Gasquet marks this whole line as unreadable, but 'dis ' can be made out from the images
me . . . amas. haec uerba erant uerisimiliter addita ad calcem paginae cum sint supra consuetum linearum 24 numerum. Nunc in ms. legi amplius nequeunt
Bianchini discipulum quem diligebat| in una linea, Irico ut supra uerisimilius
Gasquet omits the hanging line and capital 'E ', which can be clearly seen in the manuscript
tres sequentes paginae 322, 323, 324 nullum textum continent in ms., proinde incipit evangelium secundum Lucanum sine titulo.
